# Evidence - right parasagittal 4cm meningioma abutting SSS

## Clinical Questions

- What operative approach and setup best fit right parasagittal 4cm meningioma abutting SSS?
- What anatomy and imaging findings should change the plan for right parasagittal 4cm meningioma abutting SSS?
- What complications and rescue plans are most important for right parasagittal 4cm meningioma abutting SSS?

## Bottom line for this case

For right 4cm parasagittal meningioma abutting superior sagittal sinus (SSS); invasion unknown, the evidence supports a venous-preservation strategy rather than reflexively chasing maximal sinus-interface resection. Because the case input says abutting SSS and sinus invasion is unknown, confirm MRV/CTV sinus patency, wall invasion versus compression, venous lacunae, and dominant bridging/cortical veins before setting the extent-of-resection endpoint. Default bias: achieve safe extracapsular/devascularizing resection where the plane is safe, but leave planned residual and consider adjuvant radiosurgery/fractionated radiation when a patent sinus wall or critical vein would be endangered; recurrence/EOR evidence should guide follow-up and counseling after pathology and residual status are known.

## SSS / Parasagittal Surgical Outcomes

| Title | PMID/DOI | Tier | Applicability | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Optimal surgical strategy for meningiomas involving the superior sagittal sinus: a systematic review | PMID 30171502; DOI 10.1007/s10143-018-1026-1 | Systematic review | Frames surgical strategy for SSS-involving meningiomas and the tradeoff between radicality and venous morbidity. | curated PubMed pack |
| Meningiomas infiltrating the superior sagittal sinus: surgical considerations of 328 cases | PMID 16607555 | Large surgical series | Large experience focused on SSS-infiltrating meningiomas; use for operative considerations, not to overcall invasion in this case. | curated PubMed pack |
| Results with judicious modern neurosurgical management of parasagittal and falcine meningiomas | PMID 20950085; DOI 10.3171/2010.9.JNS10646 | Clinical surgical series | Supports a judicious management frame for parasagittal/falcine tumors rather than maximal sinus-risk resection by default. | curated PubMed pack |

|---|---|---|---|---|
| Venous preservation-guided resection: a changing paradigm in parasagittal meningioma surgery | PMID 23330997; DOI 10.3171/2012.11.JNS112011 | Operative strategy article | Directly supports prioritizing venous preservation, bridging/cortical vein anatomy, and safe resection limits. | curated PubMed pack |
| Classification of Peritumoral Veins in Convexity and Parasagittal Meningiomas and Its Significance in Preventing Cerebral Venous Infarction | PMID 33618045; DOI 10.1016/j.wneu.2021.02.041 | Clinical classification / cohort | Links peritumoral venous patterns to venous infarction prevention; useful for MRV/CTV and vein-preservation review. | curated PubMed pack |
| Complications after resection of parasagittal and superior sagittal sinus meningiomas | PMID 40025371; DOI 10.1007/s10143-025-03430-3 | Complications series | Recent source focused on complications after parasagittal/SSS meningioma resection. | curated PubMed pack |

| Title | PMID/DOI | Tier | Applicability | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Judicious resection and/or radiosurgery for parasagittal meningiomas: outcomes from a multicenter review | PMID 9733295 | Multicenter review | Supports considering planned residual and radiosurgery when sinus/venous risk makes aggressive resection unsafe. | curated PubMed pack |
| Meningioma involving the superior sagittal sinus: long-term outcome after robotic radiosurgery in primary and recurrent situation | PMID 37496660; DOI 10.3389/fonc.2023.1206059 | Radiosurgery cohort | Adjuvant/salvage radiosurgery option for SSS-involving or recurrent residual disease; not a reason to ignore safe surgical decompression goals. | curated PubMed pack |

| Title | PMID/DOI | Tier | Applicability | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Predictors of recurrence after surgical resection of parafalcine and parasagittal meningiomas | PMID 37987849; DOI 10.1007/s00701-023-05848-4 | Recurrence predictors cohort | Relevant to counseling and follow-up around extent of resection, residual tumor, and recurrence risk. | curated PubMed pack |
| Tumor recurrence in parasagittal and falcine atypical meningiomas invading the superior sagittal sinus | PMID 33544790; DOI 10.47162/RJME.61.2.08 | Pathology-specific recurrence series | Useful for atypical/invasive recurrence framing; do not apply unless grade/invasion is confirmed. | curated PubMed pack |

## Applicability To This Case

Apply this pack as operative framing for a parasagittal/SSS-adjacent meningioma, not as proof of sinus invasion. The practical decision is venous preservation versus incremental EOR at the sinus/bridging-vein interface.

## Uncertainty / Evidence Gaps

- Topic string says abutting SSS; do not assume true sinus wall invasion without venous imaging and operative findings.
- WHO grade, edema burden, sinus patency, venous dominance, and achieved extent of resection determine recurrence/adjuvant-therapy applicability.
- Curated pack sources need clinician confirmation against full text before being used for numeric counseling or institutional recommendations.

## Draft Sections
### Anatomy / Relevant Structures
- Classification of Peritumoral Veins in Convexity and Parasagittal Meningiomas and Its Significance in Preventing Cerebral Venous Infarction.: The medium (2-4 cm) convexity located closer to the sinus and parasagittal meningiomas (Sindou type I-Ⅲ) without obvious invasion of the superior sagittal sinus are considered simple to operate on. However, the tumors are often accompanied by the cortical bridging vein. Because of lack of collateral vein circulation in cortical areas, the damage of peritumoral veins will subsequently lead to venous infarction. To avoid the serious complications caused by intraoperative injury of peritumoral veins, it is necessary to define the classification of the progression of peritumoral veins and tumors to guide surgical safety. [pmid-33618045]
